heading: Configure Authentication with LDAP
body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

> **Note:** You can use the same Atlas CLI command to configure LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authentication and LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization.

To save one LDAP configuration for the project you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas security ldap save [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas security ldap save](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-security-ldap-save).

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

Use the following procedure to configure user authentication with
LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) for all clusters in a project.

> **Note:** You might incur additional costs when you enable this feature.
> See [Advanced Security](advanced-security).

You may list multiple servers separated by commas. You cannot use different ports.

You may provide self-signed certificates.

Wait for Atlas to deploy your changes. Atlas verifies that
your clusters can connect to, authenticate with, and query your LDAP
servers using the configuration details that you provided.
